statement: The system shall allow users to run pre‑planned movement files in a VR robot simulator to operate the manipulator.
The system shall stop all operations within one tenth of a second when any emergency stop button is pressed.
The system shall allow predefined arm moves stored in files to be triggered by pressing a button on the computer.
The system shall, upon detecting an out-of-order command, either reject the input or automatically execute the next move in the expected sequence.
The system shall be able to perform standard tasks such as aligning and screwing a nut.
The system shall allow the arm to insert a bolt regardless of its orientation using the Bolt Runner constraints feature.
The system shall have essentially zero perceptible delay; any millisecond of latency shall trigger a flag and halt operation.
The system shall break long movements into intermediate stages to allow the operator to monitor progress and see the arm's trajectory.
The system shall provide high-definition cameras for the operator view.
The system shall support up to eight camera feeds, with two primary views selectable at any time.
The system shall prioritize frame rate over resolution when bandwidth is limited.
The system shall support a large shared display, such as a 75‑inch TV, enabling multiple operators to view the same images simultaneously.
The system shall display a ghost overlay of the planned path and warn the operator if the arm deviates from it.
The system shall allow definition of no‑go zones and provide force feedback or audible alerts when the arm approaches a restricted area.
The system shall sound a buzzer when the gripper force exceeds a safe threshold to prevent over‑gripping.
The system shall include built‑in illumination LEDs in cameras to improve depth perception.
The system shall provide force‑ratio feedback or limits when handling heavy loads to reduce operator strain.
